evidence_rea: If you have left comments on the Website
an exported file of the personal data we hold about you
evidence_reg: the personal data concerning him or her, which he or she has provided to a controller
in a structured, commonly used and machine-readable format
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA narrows the scope to users who have left comments and to data the controller 'hold[s] about you', while the REG right concerns data the subject provided to the controller in a specified portable format.

evidence_rea: If you have left comments on the Website
an exported file of the personal data we hold about you
evidence_reg: The controller shall provide a copy of the personal data undergoing processing.
per_reg_comparisons.reasoning_short: The REA limits availability to a subset of users and to data the controller 'hold[s] about you', whereas the REG refers more broadly to a copy of personal data undergoing processing.
The REA does not address whether processing is based on consent or contract, so there is no direct quote-supported conflict beyond omission.
